compiled_state.title: Test quality enforcement: review overlay and pre-commit gate
compiled_state.author: Claude
compiled_state.description:
## Context

No AI code review tool in the ecosystem has built-in criteria for distinguishing meaningful tests from change-detector tests. Tests created via any path (RED test writer, fix-bug, sprint, review resolution) can introduce bloat, but the current sprint-level test review only catches tests written during sprint execution. A test quality review overlay triggered by test file changes would catch bloat regardless of origin. Additionally, a deterministic pre-commit gate using static analysis tools can detect common anti-patterns before they reach review. Research found Semgrep custom rules as the best language-agnostic option, with PyNose (Python) and eslint-plugin-jest (JS/TS) as language-specific alternatives.

## Success Criteria

- A test quality review overlay is added to the code review pipeline, triggered when the diff modifies test files, using opus to detect bloat patterns: change-detector tests, implementation-coupled assertions, tautological tests, source-file-grepping, existence-only assertions
- The redundant test review step in the sprint skill is removed, replaced by the test quality review overlay; the sprint-level removal and overlay deployment must be coordinated to prevent concurrent conflicting review mechanisms
- A configurable pre-commit test quality gate uses language-specific bloat detection tools (PyNose for Python, eslint-plugin-jest for JS/TS) with Semgrep custom rules as the language-agnostic fallback, detecting common anti-patterns in new or modified test files; the active tool is configured via dso-config.conf; the gate is calibrated to avoid false positives — it only blocks commits when there is high confidence an anti-pattern has been detected
- Semgrep is documented as a project dependency; the onboarding skill includes instructions to install Semgrep if not present (with graceful degradation when installation fails — gate disables rather than blocks) and configure language-specific test bloat detection tools for the project's languages

## Dependencies

None (most valuable after Epic 8df3-61d0, but technically independent)

## Approach

Add a test quality review overlay to the review pipeline, modeled on the existing security and performance overlays. The classifier triggers it when the diff touches test directories. The overlay uses opus with a checklist derived from the behavioral testing standard. The sprint-level test review is removed in the same story to prevent concurrent conflicting review mechanisms. The pre-commit gate uses configurable language-specific tools with Semgrep custom rules as fallback, operating only on diff-added test code. Semgrep rules target: open/cat/grep of source files in test assertions, os.path.exists/Path.exists as sole assertion, ast.parse/inspect.getsource in tests. Onboarding installs Semgrep with graceful degradation. Gate enforces a timeout budget to stay under the ~73s tool timeout ceiling.

## Research Reference

See plugins/dso/docs/designs/TESTING_RESEARCH.md and plugins/dso/docs/designs/TESTING_SESSION.md for full research findings and session context.
